beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, and light breakfast at home.
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit; she's restless after two busy shifts and wants to get moving. The mild 68F weather is perfect for an outdoor morning run.

beats.activity: Trail run around Lake Union with film camera.
beats.reasoning: Her signature Monday–Thursday morning ritual; the partly cloudy weather is ideal for photography without harsh shadows. Fitness obsession + camera everywhere = this is non-negotiable.

beats.activity: Drive to Harborview; transit strike means rideshare surge, so she opts for her car.
beats.reasoning: Metro buses and Link suspended until 4pm citywide; she drives to avoid surge pricing. Leaving early to beat traffic and get settled before shift.

beats.activity: Morning ER shift: triage, intake, patient care.
beats.reasoning: Standard ER morning shift. Thursday is typically moderate-busy; she's mentally prepared after two intense recent days.

beats.activity: Lunch break: salad at Pike Place Market, brief wandering.
beats.reasoning: Her go-to lunch spot for quiet reflection after busy mornings. Same location as work, so minimal travel time. Values the market's calm corners.

beats.activity: Afternoon ER shift: patient care, intake.
beats.reasoning: Completes her standard Thursday ER day. Clocking out at 6pm means she can avoid peak surge pricing as transit restarts at 4pm.

beats.activity: Drive from Harborview to Capitol Hill food bank.
beats.location_name: Capitol Hill Food Bank (Capitol Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Transit is back online at 4pm, but she prefers driving tonight to stick to her routine. Across-town drive; she's committed to Thursday volunteering despite the strike.

beats.activity: Food bank sorting and donation organization.
beats.location_name: Capitol Hill Food Bank (Capitol Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Weekly volunteering commitment. Gives her purpose beyond the ER; she finds this grounding after high-stress shifts. Mild evening weather makes the commute feel less draining.
Cross-town drive back to Leschi. She's tired but fulfilled after a full day of work and volunteering.

beats.activity: Dinner, organize vinyl collection, review film from today's run.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down routine: she makes a simple meal, spends time with her records and film camera work. Low-key evening after an active day. Preparing for sleep.
7 hours of sleep; she's earned it after a full shift and volunteering. Ends before 6am as required, ready to wake refreshed for Friday.

diary: Started before dawn with coffee and a trail run around Lake Union—brought my film camera, caught some good light through the trees. The transit strike meant I had to drive to Harborview, which saved me the headache of surge pricing rideshare, at least. ER was steady all morning, usual triage chaos, but nothing too heavy. Grabbed a salad at Pike Place around noon and just wandered for a bit, watching the buskers. Afternoon shift blurred together the way they do. After clocking out, I drove over to Capitol Hill Food Bank instead of heading straight home—felt like I needed to do something that mattered today. Spent an hour sorting donations, organizing boxes. There's something grounding about that work. Now I'm home with my vinyls, reviewing the film negatives from this morning's run. The light was really something special.
thought_process: The strike actually worked in my favor—I could've stressed about transit delays, but driving my own car meant control and quiet time before a long shift. Work was routine enough that I didn't feel drained, so when I finished, instead of collapsing, I had energy for the food bank. I think I needed that today. The morning run with the camera was deliberate; I've been wanting to shoot more film, and the light was right. My mood stayed steady—not high, not low, just present. The evening reviewing negatives felt like a good way to close out, something creative and slow.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
